customer_anxiety_profile: Low anxiety, high practicality. Ken is not skeptical of solar — he already owns a system. His constraint is logistical (was driving during the call) rather than emotional. He will engage if the callback is timely and the specialist demonstrates knowledge of his specific situation.
unresolved_concerns: Bill amount unconfirmed — Ken was driving and could only offer a ballpark; $200/month figure from lead record was disputed as 'a little high'
No locked callback time — Ken said 'a couple hours' but no specific time was agreed
Email address not confirmed on-call — Ken agreed to send the bill by email but Holland did not verify the address
sensitive_topics_raised: Existing system underperforming due to suboptimal panel placement caused by large backyard tree — Ken volunteered this; it is an emotional anchor available at opener
exact_promises_rep_made: Holland agreed to send Ken an email so Ken could reply with his utility bill
Holland agreed to 'pick it up in a couple hours'
closer_should_know: Ken's existing Astrum system is placed suboptimally because of a large backyard tree — he knows it and said so unprompted. That frustration is the emotional opener for the add-on conversation. Lead in with 'I understand your current panels aren't in the best position because of the tree — that's exactly what we want to address with the design.' Do not treat this as a generic add-on inquiry; Ken has a specific performance problem he wants solved.
